verses.text: Luke writes 'in order' to Theophilus, that he may know 'the certainty' of these things. Careful witness. The gospel rests on diligent, eyewitness testimony.
Luke writes 'in order' to Theophilus, that he may know 'the certainty' of these things. Careful witness. The gospel rests on diligent, eyewitness testimony.
Luke writes 'in order' to Theophilus, that he may know 'the certainty' of these things. Careful witness. The gospel rests on diligent, eyewitness testimony.
Luke writes 'in order' to Theophilus, that he may know 'the certainty' of these things. Careful witness. The gospel rests on diligent, eyewitness testimony.
Zacharias and Elisabeth are righteous yet childless and old. Long waiting. God's best gifts sometimes come after years of patient prayer.
Zacharias and Elisabeth are righteous yet childless and old. Long waiting. God's best gifts sometimes come after years of patient prayer.
Zacharias and Elisabeth are righteous yet childless and old. Long waiting. God's best gifts sometimes come after years of patient prayer.
As he burns incense, an angel appears, and Zacharias is troubled and afraid. Heaven breaks in. The presence of God's messenger fills a man with awe.
As he burns incense, an angel appears, and Zacharias is troubled and afraid. Heaven breaks in. The presence of God's messenger fills a man with awe.
As he burns incense, an angel appears, and Zacharias is troubled and afraid. Heaven breaks in. The presence of God's messenger fills a man with awe.
As he burns incense, an angel appears, and Zacharias is troubled and afraid. Heaven breaks in. The presence of God's messenger fills a man with awe.
As he burns incense, an angel appears, and Zacharias is troubled and afraid. Heaven breaks in. The presence of God's messenger fills a man with awe.
'Fear not... thy prayer is heard'; Elisabeth shall bear John, great before the Lord. Answered prayer. The petition long offered is granted at last.
'Fear not... thy prayer is heard'; Elisabeth shall bear John, great before the Lord. Answered prayer. The petition long offered is granted at last.
'Fear not... thy prayer is heard'; Elisabeth shall bear John, great before the Lord. Answered prayer. The petition long offered is granted at last.
He shall turn many to the Lord, 'in the spirit... of Elias,' to make ready a people. The forerunner. John's task is to prepare hearts for Christ.
He shall turn many to the Lord, 'in the spirit... of Elias,' to make ready a people. The forerunner. John's task is to prepare hearts for Christ.
Zacharias doubts for his age and is struck dumb till it is fulfilled. Unbelief rebuked. Even a righteous man's doubt is gently corrected.
Zacharias doubts for his age and is struck dumb till it is fulfilled. Unbelief rebuked. Even a righteous man's doubt is gently corrected.
Zacharias doubts for his age and is struck dumb till it is fulfilled. Unbelief rebuked. Even a righteous man's doubt is gently corrected.
The people marvel at his delay; he comes out speechless; Elisabeth conceives and hides herself. Wonder confirmed. The sign Zacharias asked is given in his silence.
The people marvel at his delay; he comes out speechless; Elisabeth conceives and hides herself. Wonder confirmed. The sign Zacharias asked is given in his silence.
The people marvel at his delay; he comes out speechless; Elisabeth conceives and hides herself. Wonder confirmed. The sign Zacharias asked is given in his silence.
The people marvel at his delay; he comes out speechless; Elisabeth conceives and hides herself. Wonder confirmed. The sign Zacharias asked is given in his silence.
The people marvel at his delay; he comes out speechless; Elisabeth conceives and hides herself. Wonder confirmed. The sign Zacharias asked is given in his silence.
Gabriel comes to Mary, 'Hail, thou that art highly favoured'; she is troubled at the saying. Surprised by grace. The lowly maid is greeted as favoured of God.
Gabriel comes to Mary, 'Hail, thou that art highly favoured'; she is troubled at the saying. Surprised by grace. The lowly maid is greeted as favoured of God.
Gabriel comes to Mary, 'Hail, thou that art highly favoured'; she is troubled at the saying. Surprised by grace. The lowly maid is greeted as favoured of God.
Gabriel comes to Mary, 'Hail, thou that art highly favoured'; she is troubled at the saying. Surprised by grace. The lowly maid is greeted as favoured of God.
'Fear not... thou shalt... bring forth a son... JESUS'; He shall be the Son of the Highest and reign for ever. The promised King. Mary's child is the eternal Son and heir of David's throne.
'Fear not... thou shalt... bring forth a son... JESUS'; He shall be the Son of the Highest and reign for ever. The promised King. Mary's child is the eternal Son and heir of David's throne.
'Fear not... thou shalt... bring forth a son... JESUS'; He shall be the Son of the Highest and reign for ever. The promised King. Mary's child is the eternal Son and heir of David's throne.
'Fear not... thou shalt... bring forth a son... JESUS'; He shall be the Son of the Highest and reign for ever. The promised King. Mary's child is the eternal Son and heir of David's throne.
How shall this be? 'The Holy Ghost shall come upon thee'; the holy child shall be called the Son of God. The virgin birth. Christ is conceived by the Spirit's power, truly God and man.
How shall this be? 'The Holy Ghost shall come upon thee'; the holy child shall be called the Son of God. The virgin birth. Christ is conceived by the Spirit's power, truly God and man.
Elisabeth too has conceived, 'for with God nothing shall be impossible'; Mary says, 'be it unto me.' Humble faith. She yields herself wholly to God's word.
Elisabeth too has conceived, 'for with God nothing shall be impossible'; Mary says, 'be it unto me.' Humble faith. She yields herself wholly to God's word.
Elisabeth too has conceived, 'for with God nothing shall be impossible'; Mary says, 'be it unto me.' Humble faith. She yields herself wholly to God's word.
Mary visits Elisabeth, whose babe leaps; filled with the Spirit, she blesses Mary. Joyful greeting. The unborn John rejoices at the coming of his Lord.
Mary visits Elisabeth, whose babe leaps; filled with the Spirit, she blesses Mary. Joyful greeting. The unborn John rejoices at the coming of his Lord.
Mary visits Elisabeth, whose babe leaps; filled with the Spirit, she blesses Mary. Joyful greeting. The unborn John rejoices at the coming of his Lord.
Mary visits Elisabeth, whose babe leaps; filled with the Spirit, she blesses Mary. Joyful greeting. The unborn John rejoices at the coming of his Lord.
'Whence is this to me, that the mother of my Lord should come?' Blessed is she that believed. Honoured faith. Elisabeth praises Mary's trust in God's word.
'Whence is this to me, that the mother of my Lord should come?' Blessed is she that believed. Honoured faith. Elisabeth praises Mary's trust in God's word.
'Whence is this to me, that the mother of my Lord should come?' Blessed is she that believed. Honoured faith. Elisabeth praises Mary's trust in God's word.
'My soul doth magnify the Lord'; He that is mighty hath done great things, and His mercy is on them that fear Him. The Magnificat. Mary's song exalts the God who lifts the lowly.
'My soul doth magnify the Lord'; He that is mighty hath done great things, and His mercy is on them that fear Him. The Magnificat. Mary's song exalts the God who lifts the lowly.
'My soul doth magnify the Lord'; He that is mighty hath done great things, and His mercy is on them that fear Him. The Magnificat. Mary's song exalts the God who lifts the lowly.
'My soul doth magnify the Lord'; He that is mighty hath done great things, and His mercy is on them that fear Him. The Magnificat. Mary's song exalts the God who lifts the lowly.
'My soul doth magnify the Lord'; He that is mighty hath done great things, and His mercy is on them that fear Him. The Magnificat. Mary's song exalts the God who lifts the lowly.
He scatters the proud, puts down the mighty, exalts the lowly, fills the hungry, and sends the rich empty away. Great reversal. God overturns the world's order in mercy.
He scatters the proud, puts down the mighty, exalts the lowly, fills the hungry, and sends the rich empty away. Great reversal. God overturns the world's order in mercy.
He scatters the proud, puts down the mighty, exalts the lowly, fills the hungry, and sends the rich empty away. Great reversal. God overturns the world's order in mercy.
He has helped Israel, remembering His mercy to Abraham; Mary stays three months. Promise kept. God's ancient covenant comes to fulfilment in Christ.
He has helped Israel, remembering His mercy to Abraham; Mary stays three months. Promise kept. God's ancient covenant comes to fulfilment in Christ.
He has helped Israel, remembering His mercy to Abraham; Mary stays three months. Promise kept. God's ancient covenant comes to fulfilment in Christ.
Elisabeth bears a son; they would name him Zacharias, but she says, 'He shall be called John.' Obedient naming. They follow the word of the angel, not custom.
Elisabeth bears a son; they would name him Zacharias, but she says, 'He shall be called John.' Obedient naming. They follow the word of the angel, not custom.
Elisabeth bears a son; they would name him Zacharias, but she says, 'He shall be called John.' Obedient naming. They follow the word of the angel, not custom.
Elisabeth bears a son; they would name him Zacharias, but she says, 'He shall be called John.' Obedient naming. They follow the word of the angel, not custom.
Zacharias writes, 'His name is John'; his mouth is opened, and he praises God. Tongue loosed. Obedience restores the speech that doubt had silenced.
Zacharias writes, 'His name is John'; his mouth is opened, and he praises God. Tongue loosed. Obedience restores the speech that doubt had silenced.
Zacharias writes, 'His name is John'; his mouth is opened, and he praises God. Tongue loosed. Obedience restores the speech that doubt had silenced.
Zacharias writes, 'His name is John'; his mouth is opened, and he praises God. Tongue loosed. Obedience restores the speech that doubt had silenced.
Fear comes on all; 'what manner of child shall this be?' for the Lord's hand was with him. Wondering awe. God's dealings stir the whole neighbourhood.
Fear comes on all; 'what manner of child shall this be?' for the Lord's hand was with him. Wondering awe. God's dealings stir the whole neighbourhood.
Zacharias prophesies: 'Blessed be the Lord... for he hath visited and redeemed his people,' raising a horn of salvation. The Benedictus. He praises God for the dawning redemption.
Zacharias prophesies: 'Blessed be the Lord... for he hath visited and redeemed his people,' raising a horn of salvation. The Benedictus. He praises God for the dawning redemption.
Zacharias prophesies: 'Blessed be the Lord... for he hath visited and redeemed his people,' raising a horn of salvation. The Benedictus. He praises God for the dawning redemption.

Zacharias prophesies: 'Blessed be the Lord... for he hath visited and redeemed his people,' raising a horn of salvation. The Benedictus. He praises God for the dawning redemption.
God performs the mercy and oath sworn to Abraham, that His people, delivered, might serve Him without fear. Covenant fulfilled. Redemption frees us to serve God in holiness.
God performs the mercy and oath sworn to Abraham, that His people, delivered, might serve Him without fear. Covenant fulfilled. Redemption frees us to serve God in holiness.
God performs the mercy and oath sworn to Abraham, that His people, delivered, might serve Him without fear. Covenant fulfilled. Redemption frees us to serve God in holiness.
God performs the mercy and oath sworn to Abraham, that His people, delivered, might serve Him without fear. Covenant fulfilled. Redemption frees us to serve God in holiness.
'And thou, child, shalt be... the prophet of the Highest,' giving knowledge of salvation by remission of sins. John's calling. He points to the pardon Christ would bring.
'And thou, child, shalt be... the prophet of the Highest,' giving knowledge of salvation by remission of sins. John's calling. He points to the pardon Christ would bring.
Through 'the tender mercy of our God,' the dayspring from on high visits us; the child grew. Dawning light. Christ rises like the morning on a world in darkness.
Through 'the tender mercy of our God,' the dayspring from on high visits us; the child grew. Dawning light. Christ rises like the morning on a world in darkness.
